title: Aureline marketplace discovery-result, ranking-reason-chip, and ranking-floor vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for marketplace discovery surfaces. Pins the discovery-result row, the ranking-reason chip, and the ranking-floor order used by every catalog browse, search, and install-review surface so trust, provenance, compatibility, runtime-cost, safety / policy, and bridge-state language never fragments across public, private, mirror, offline-bundle, and local-archive lanes. Reuses the registry / mirror / offline-bundle vocabulary frozen by schemas/extensions/registry_manifest.schema.json (registry_source_class, channel_class, approval_state_class, trust_claim_source, trust_badge_inheritance_rule, compatibility_claim_class, anti_abuse_signal_class, revocation_snapshot_age_class) and the runtime-budget / quarantine vocabulary frozen by artifacts/extensions/runtime_budget_rows.yaml and artifacts/extensions/quarantine_rules.yaml. Discovery surfaces project these classes into chips and a deterministic ranking-floor order; they MUST NOT mint marketplace-shaped fields ad hoc. Adding a new chip class, ranking-floor class, runtime-cost class, bridge-state class, or position class is additive-minor with a discovery_ranking_reason_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw artifact bytes, raw signing-key material, raw attestation-bundle bytes, raw URLs, raw publisher-private data, raw repository paths, and raw popularity counts MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs and class labels only.

discovery_ranking_reason_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this discovery-ranking-reason vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

extension_identity.description: Namespaced extension id of the form 'publisher_id/extension_id'. Mirrors ADR-0012 / registry_manifest.schema.json.

semver_string.description: SemVer string the discovery row resolves to. Pinning is a policy-pack concern; this field carries the declared candidate version.

registry_source_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/registry_source_class verbatim. A discovery surface MUST NOT mint a parallel registry-source vocabulary.

channel_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/channel_class. Discovery surfaces SHOULD only render production rows by default; approved and quarantine rows render with an explicit channel chip.

approval_state_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/approval_state_class. revoked / emergency_disabled / blocked_by_policy rows MUST be rendered with the corresponding warning class from discovery_badge.schema.json.

trust_claim_source.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/trust_claim_source. Discovery surfaces render this value as an explicit provenance chip; silent fallback to 'verified_publisher' for non-origin sources is forbidden.

trust_badge_inheritance_rule.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/trust_badge_inheritance_rule. The rendered publisher-trust badge on a discovery row reads this rule rather than the origin row's tier directly.

revocation_snapshot_age_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/revocation_snapshot_age_class. A 'stale' or 'unverified_no_snapshot' row MUST render the corresponding warning class on discovery; silent inheritance of the origin trust tier is forbidden.

anti_abuse_signal_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/anti_abuse_signal_class. A discovery surface that hides a raised signal is denied with review_disclosure_incomplete.

runtime_cost_class.description: Closed runtime-cost vocabulary projected from the extension-runtime budget axes (artifacts/extensions/runtime_budget_rows.yaml). 'runtime_cost_unknown_pending_evidence' is the honest default for a row that has not produced an activation-evidence packet yet; surfaces MUST NOT promote 'runtime_cost_unknown_pending_evidence' to 'runtime_cost_low_nominal' on the basis of popularity or self-reported counters alone.

runtime_cost_evidence_class.description: Closed evidence-class vocabulary backing the runtime-cost class. self_reported_only_unverified MUST NOT credit a 'runtime_cost_low_nominal' class on its own; the supporting evidence MUST come from an activation-evidence packet or a benchmark archive.

bridge_state_class.description: Closed bridge-state vocabulary. Discovery and detail surfaces render exactly one bridge-state class per result; install-review reads the same value rather than minting per-surface bridge labels.

ranking_floor_class.description: Closed ranking-floor vocabulary. The default ranking_floor_order on every discovery surface is [safety_first, compatibility_first, maintainership_first, runtime_cost_first, policy_pinned_first, secondary_popularity]; a chip on the diagnostic_only_no_score floor never moves a row up or down. Surfaces that promote popularity ahead of safety / compatibility / maintainership / runtime-cost are denied with discovery_ranking_floor_violated.

ranking_reason_chip_class.description: Closed ranking-reason chip vocabulary. The chip set is authoritative; discovery, details, and install-review surfaces project exactly these chips. Surfaces MUST NOT mint marketplace-shaped chip names ad hoc.

chip_polarity.description: Polarity of the chip's contribution to its ranking_floor. 'suppress_only' chips never raise a row's score; they only narrow a result set or move a row out of ranking.

discovery_position_class.description: Closed discovery-position vocabulary. The position is computed from the chip set and the ranking-floor order; surfaces render the position as a chip and MUST NOT silently move a row out of 'removed_from_ranking_visible_in_detail_only' or 'not_in_ranking_review_only' on the basis of popularity.

redaction_class.description: Mirrors registry_manifest.schema.json#/$defs/redaction_class. Raw artifact bytes, raw URLs, raw repository paths, raw popularity counts, raw publisher-private fields MUST NOT appear on any sink.

rendered_label.description: Human-legible label rendered on the discovery / detail / install-review surface. Raw publisher-private material forbidden.

evidence_refs.description: Refs to the rows that justify this chip (registry_manifest_row, mirror_continuity_row, publisher_continuity_row, activation-evidence packet id, policy_pack_constraint_row, anti_abuse_signal_entry source). Raw evidence bodies never carried.

explanation_label.description: Inspectable explanation rendered when the user opens the chip. Raw publisher-private material forbidden.

ranking_reason_chip_entry.description: One ranking-reason chip on a discovery row. Chips are inspectable: a surface that hides the explanation when the user opens the chip is denied with review_disclosure_incomplete.

ranking_floor_order.description: Ordered list of ranking-floor classes rendered on the surface. The default order is [ranking_floor_safety_first, ranking_floor_compatibility_first, ranking_floor_maintainership_first, ranking_floor_runtime_cost_first, ranking_floor_policy_pinned_first, ranking_secondary_popularity]; a surface MAY narrow it (drop popularity, drop policy_pinned for unmanaged workspaces) but MUST NOT promote ranking_secondary_popularity above any of the four safety / compatibility / maintainership / runtime-cost floors.

ranking_reason_chip_entries.description: At least one ranking-reason chip. A row with no chips is non-conforming; honesty demands a 'compatibility_unknown_pending_reverification' or 'runtime_cost_unknown_pending_evidence' chip over an empty list.

registry_manifest_row_ref.description: Ref to the registry_manifest_row this discovery row resolves against. Raw artifact bytes, raw URLs, and raw repository paths never carried here.

publisher_continuity_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0012 publisher_continuity_row for the publisher.

policy_pack_applicable_refs.description: Refs to ADR-0012 policy_pack_constraint_row rows whose scope covers this discovery row.

discovery_badge_record_refs.description: Refs to discovery_badge_record / discovery_warning_record rows from schemas/extensions/discovery_badge.schema.json that render on this row's card.

anti_abuse_signal_class_set_observed.description: Anti-abuse signal classes observed on the underlying registry_manifest_row, projected here so a discovery surface can render them as chips and warnings without re-deriving them.

allOf.description: A row whose approval_state_class is revoked / emergency_disabled / blocked_by_policy MUST NOT render in the 'promoted_safe_maintained_compatible_low_cost' or 'elevated_compatibility_match' positions; it is at most 'demoted_warning_visible', 'removed_from_ranking_visible_in_detail_only', or 'not_in_ranking_review_only'.

A row whose trust_badge_inheritance_rule is quarantined_cannot_inherit MUST NOT render in 'promoted_safe_maintained_compatible_low_cost' and MUST carry a 'publisher_quarantined_cannot_inherit' chip in ranking_reason_chip_entries.

A row whose runtime_cost_class is runtime_cost_quarantined_under_crash_loop_or_egress_breach MUST be removed_from_ranking_visible_in_detail_only or not_in_ranking_review_only and MUST carry a 'runtime_cost_quarantined' chip plus a 'removed_from_ranking' chip.

A row whose runtime_cost_class is runtime_cost_low_nominal MUST cite runtime_cost_evidence_class in {activation_evidence_packet_present, benchmark_archive_present}; a self-reported counter alone may not credit a low-cost chip.

A row whose channel_class is quarantine MUST be at most 'suppressed_below_compatible_match', 'demoted_warning_visible', 'removed_from_ranking_visible_in_detail_only', or 'not_in_ranking_review_only' and MUST carry a 'suppressed_in_ranking' or 'removed_from_ranking' chip.
ranking_floor_order MUST start with one of the four ranking floors safety_first / compatibility_first / maintainership_first / runtime_cost_first; popularity may never be the first floor.

discovery_result_row.description: One ranked discovery-result row. Discovery, details, and install-review surfaces project the same row; minting per-surface ranking-reason vocabulary is non-conforming.

subject_discovery_result_row_ref.description: Ref to the discovery_result_row this chip annotates.

explanation_label.description: Inspectable explanation rendered when the user opens the chip.

ranking_reason_chip_record.description: Standalone ranking-reason chip record. Used when a chip needs to be cited from a journal entry, support export, or claim manifest independently of the parent discovery_result_row payload.
